articles.title: Police searching for suspect after more than 80 cars damaged across several NJ towns
articles.article_text: --- title: Police searching for suspect after more than 80 cars damaged across several NJ towns url: https://abc7ny.com/post/police-working-coordinated-search-suspect-80-cars-vandalized-nj-towns/18853350/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Police now say more than 80 vehicles were damaged in a wild vandalism spree across Middlesex and Union counties. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18853350', 'vandalism,car,Rahway'] --- NEW JERSEY (WABC) -- Police now say more than 80 vehicles were damaged in a wild vandalism spree across Middlesex and Union counties. Vehicles in Cranford, Linden and Rahway had their windows smashed by bricks or pavers. Police are working together to find a man who was seen on surveillance video throwing the pavers onto car windows. A new window is lowered onto Anita Kornagay's car on Tuesday afternoon after someone threw a heavy stone paver onto her windshield, shattering it. Kornagay is one of 11 whose vehicles were vandalized at the Rosegate Heritage Village 55-plus community on East Hazelwood Avenue in Rahway. "I gotta pay, what? 370. And that's a lot of money that I didn't have, you know. So why would you come to the seniors' building? We don't have that kind of money, you know," Kornagay said. Her neighbor, Alma Small, also one of more than 80 victims of this string of vandalism across Union and Middlesex Counties between 1am and 4am Monday. "It made me angry. I was angry about it, you know, because it's. It's not right. I mean, why what's the purpose of it? You know, making people spend money that they don't have," Small said. Police shared surveillance images of a man and a dark grey Nissan Rogue, which they say he drove around carrying the massive grey stone pavers an d tossed onto random cars. Now, multiple towns, including Woodbridge, Edison and Hillside, are working together to find the suspect. Linden Police say they are investigating more than two dozen vehicles damaged and Cranford looking into 8 instances. "It's a violent act. And while property was damaged, it's still an act of violence to that property, and that's unacceptable. And people should rest their heads on their pillow every night, not worrying about their neighborhood being disturbed by someone who wanted to create harm," Cranford Police Chief Matthew Nazzaro said. articles.title: Taylor Frankie Paul files counter restraining order against ex
articles.author: Jami Ganz
articles.article_text: --- title: Taylor Frankie Paul files counter restraining order against ex author: Jami Ganz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/07/taylor-frankie-paul-counter-restraining-order-dakota-mortensen/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Taylor Frankie Paul has filed a restraining order against Dakota Mortensen, who currently has one against her, as a judge handed down a temporary custody arrangement for the reality stars. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['SNYDE'] --- Taylor Frankie Paul has filed a counter temporary restraining order against ex Dakota Mortensen — both of whom have accused each other of recent domestic violence — as a judge issued a temporary custody arrangement dictating how much time she’s allotted with their toddler son. The 31-year-old “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star’s filing was confirmed Tuesday by Us Weekly. People reports the TRO is also on behalf of the exes’ son Ever, 2, with Paul citing an alleged “high number of instances of abuse and domestic violence (assault, harassment, and stalking) at Dakota’s hands.” The same day, a Utah judge ruled that the would-be “Bachelorette” can see Ever for up to 8 weekly hours of supervised parent time, which can be broken up into two- or three-hour blocks, until an April 30 court hearing, TMZ reports. The fifth season of Hulu’s “Secret Lives” halted production last month amid reports that Utah officials were investigating allegations Paul recently attacked Mortensen, 33. Both were ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluations. Days before Paul’s “Bachelorette” season was set to premiere on ABC, TMZ published video from the 2023 domestic violence incident that resulted in Paul’s notorious arrest. The clip shows a drunken Paul screaming at Mortensen as she tries to put him in a chokehold before chucking three metal bar stools at him, one of which seemed to hit her daughter Indy, 5. Paul shares the little girl, now 8, with ex-husband Tate Paul, with whom she also shares a 5 year old son named Ocean. Paul’s arrest opened the “Mormon Wives” pilot and it was well-publicized that she pleaded guilty to felony aggravated assault in exchange for the state tossing charges of domestic violence in the presence of a child, child abuse, and criminal mischief. But the video of the inciting incident was all ABC needed to pull the plug on “The Bachelorette.” That same day, Mortensen filed a protective order against Paul and was promptly awarded temporary custody of Ever.

articles.title: Griffin Kitchen & Bar to open in Roslyn
articles.author: Erica Marcus
articles.article_text: --- title: Griffin Kitchen & Bar to open in Roslyn author: Erica Marcus url: https://www.newsday.com/lifestyle/restaurants/griffin-kitchen-and-bar-roslyn-l180niug hostname: newsday.com description: The chef-owner of the American bistro hopes to break the address' curse. sitename: Newsday date: 2026-04-05 categories: ['Things to Do'] tags: ['Restaurants, FeedMe'] --- No restaurant has really worked at 1512 Old Northern Blvd., but Pasquale Abatiello is determined to break the curse with his upcoming Griffin Kitchen & Bar. The location would seem to have everything going for it: Roslyn is one of Nassau’s premier dining destinations, this attractive spot is in the same shopping center as two village stalwarts, Kotobuki and Besito, and it has loads of parking. And yet neither of the first two food ventures in the former salon space — Cool Mess (est. 2018), an ice-cream-focused eatery, and Limani Mezze (2021), a (slightly) lower-priced offshoot of the Greek original a mile west — made it past the three-year mark. Last year, a sign went up announcing the arrival of Mia’s, but it was taken down before the offshoot of the Brooklyn-based bakery opened. Enter Abatiello, a Long Island native who worked at Porto Vivo and Red in Huntington and Barrique in Babylon before heading north to Westchester, where he was the executive chef of the Wood & Fire group, one of whose six restaurants, Southern Table in Pleasantville, is featured in the Michelin Guide. A new father of twins, he decided it was time to work closer to home and, for the first time, become an owner as well as a chef. Initially he had been considering an Italian menu but, as he assessed the Roslyn landscape (Felice, Alto Enoteca, Trattoria Diane, and a forthcoming Poll brothers Italian steak concept), he decided on a more global concept that ranges from buttermilk biscuits, mussels a la plancha and Wagyu meatballs to schnitzel with chicory-arugula salad, braised short ribs with potato puree, grass-fed burgers, fried chicken sandwiches and dry-aged steaks. At Abatiello’s side in the kitchen is another Long Islander, Frances Cappi, who most recently cooked in Manhattan at Jean-Georges Vongerichten’s ABC Kitchen. Just as impressive: His first food job was at Ceriello’s Fine Foods, the great Italian specialty market in Williston Park. Griffin’s main dining room will seat about 75, with 36 more at the bar / lounge and another 30 outside on the patio. Pasquale said he hopes to open by mid-April.

articles.title: Rodeph Sholom School - Upper East Side, NY Patch
articles.article_text: --- title: Rodeph Sholom School - Upper East Side, NY Patch url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-west-side-nyc/business/listing/556983/rodeph-sholom-school hostname: patch.com description: Rodeph Sholom School's Nursery through 8th Grade program cultivates curious, confident learners in the heart of Manhattan's Upper West Side. Our... sitename: Upper East Side, NY Patch date: 2025-11-15 categories: ['Business Listing'] --- About Us Rodeph Sholom School's Nursery through 8th Grade program cultivates curious, confident learners in the heart of Manhattan's Upper West Side. Our educational philosophy centers on knowing each child deeply—understanding their strengths and challenges to create personalized pathways for growth. Research from ACT demonstrates that academic achievement by 8th Grade has a larger impact on college and career readiness than high school performance—underscoring why our N-8 model matters. From Early Childhood through Middle School, we blend academic rigor with responsive teaching that meets students where they are. In our youngest years, we foster social-emotional development and executive function skills during the period most critical for brain development. Elementary students build mastery in core academics while developing the critical thinking abilities that prepare them for increasingly complex work ahead. Through Middle School, faculty collaborate closely to design differentiated instruction that challenges every child appropriately. Research-based pedagogies help students master problem-solving and effective communication across all subjects. What distinguishes Rodeph Sholom School is our commitment to the whole child. Strong social-emotional foundations enable academic risk-taking and intellectual growth. Students develop self-awareness, find their voice, and express insights through writing, discussion, creative work, and presentations to classmates, parents, and the broader community. Our mission extends beyond high test scores: we develop each student's love of learning and sense of responsibility to themselves and others. Through meaningful experiences—from buddy programs pairing older and younger students to extended studies across disciplines—children discover their capabilities while building lasting connections. At Rodeph Sholom School, today’s curious learners become tomorrow's purpose-driven leaders. Contact Admissions: admissions@rssnyc.org NEW Community Endorsements Community Endorsements NEW

articles.title: Iran won’t compete at Milan Cortina Paralympics as its only athlete can’t travel safely to Italy
articles.author: Associated Press
articles.article_text: --- title: Iran won’t compete at Milan Cortina Paralympics as its only athlete can’t travel safely to Italy author: Associated Press url: https://www.mercurynews.com/2026/03/06/iran-paralympics/ hostname: mercurynews.com description: Aboulfazl Khatibi was set to compete in two Para cross-country events beginning next week. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-03-06 categories: ['Olympics'] tags: ['Iran Paralympics, Winter Paralympics, Aboulfazl Khatibi'] --- CORTINA D’AMPEZZO, Italy (AP) — Iran will not participate in the Milan Cortina Paralympics because its only athlete set to compete can’t safely travel to Italy amid the intensifying Middle East conflict, the International Paralympic Committee said Friday. The announcement that Aboulfazl Khatibi won’t compete came just hours ahead of the opening ceremony in Verona. The Games are beginning less than a week since the United States and Israel launched a military attack against Iran. The IPC said that “due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Pyeongchang 2018 and Beijing 2022 Paralympian cannot travel safely to Italy.” IPC president Andrew Parsons said Paralympic organizers had been “working tirelessly behind the scenes” with the Iranian National Paralympic Committee to “find alternative routes for the safe passage of the Iran delegation to the Games.” He said, however, that “with the conflict ongoing across the Middle East, the risk to human life is too high.” Parsons said dialogue with the Iranian Paralympic Committee was not easy because communication systems were down across much of the nation. “We were informed by NPC Iran that safe passage to Milano Cortina 2026 was not possible, and, as a result, they would not be able to come to the Games,” Parsons said. “To not compete at a Paralympic Winter Games because of factors outside of his control after years of training and dedication is heartbreaking for the athlete and our sympathies are with Aboulfazl at this difficult time.” Khatibi was set to compete in two Para cross-country events beginning next week. He had been announced as Iran’s flagbearer but was not going to actually carry the flag — volunteers will be handed the task for all nations because not all flagbearers will be able to attend the ceremony for logistics and training issues. The IPC said Iran’s flag was removed from the nations’ parade at the opening ceremony on Friday. AP Winter Paralympics: https://apnews.com/hub/paralympic-games

articles.title: UES Subway Station Rolls Out Accessibility Upgrades
articles.author: Miranda Levingston
articles.article_text: --- title: UES Subway Station Rolls Out Accessibility Upgrades author: Miranda Levingston url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-east-side-nyc/ues-subway-station-unveils-accessibility-upgrades hostname: patch.com description: Getting around just got more seamless for subway riders with strollers, heavy bags, or mobility issues. sitename: Patch date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['Traffic & Transit'] --- UES Subway Station Rolls Out Accessibility Upgrades Getting around just got more seamless for subway riders with strollers, heavy bags, or mobility issues. UPPER EAST SIDE, NY — Three new escalators have been installed at an Upper East Side subway station, as part of an ongoing modernization effort of the New York City Subway system. The new escalators are located on the mezzanine and platforms of the Lexington Avenue-East 63rd Street subway station, which serves the F line. The escalators were activated on Monday. Three other stations also received updated escalators and elevators throughout the subway system on Tuesday, according to the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, including Union Square, 145th Street, and the B/Q/R station at DeKalb Avenue. Find out what's happening in Upper East Sidefor free with the latest updates from Patch. In total, the upgrades span 11 different lines. "Today's completion of upgraded elevators and escalators at 14 St-Union Sq and Lexington Av-63 St — both in my congressional district — is exactly the kind of proactive investment our system needs," Congressman Jerry Nadler said. Find out what's happening in Upper East Sidefor free with the latest updates from Patch. "By replacing aging equipment before it fails, the MTA is delivering on its promise of a more reliable and inclusive transit system for all New Yorkers." The upgrades were financed through New York's capital project funding, MTA CEO Janno Lieber said. "MTA’s accessibility agenda involves more than just building brand new," Lieber said. "It’s equally important to replace older, existing elevators before they break down and leave customers stranded, and thanks to Governor Hochul we now have the money to do that." Manhattan's next elevator replacements will be at the West 72nd Street station, the West 4th Street station, and Union Square. For questions, email Miranda.Levingston@Patch.com. Get more local news delivered straight to your inbox. Sign up for free Patch newsletters and alerts.

articles.title: Want to go solar, but worried by NYC's recent landmark lawsuit? Here's what to know.
articles.author: Mahsa Saeidi; Walter Smith Randolph
articles.article_text: --- title: Want to go solar, but worried by NYC's recent landmark lawsuit? Here's what to know. author: Mahsa Saeidi; Walter Smith Randolph url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/solar-power-what-to-know-nyc/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Here's how New Yorkers can go solar while protecting their pocketbooks. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-03-13 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Solar Power'] --- Last month, New York City announced that CBS News New York's investigative team .Now, investigative reporter Mahsa Saeidi is building on that reporting with a roadmap on how you can go solar, while protecting your pocketbook. Cautionary tale thought solar power would free her from skyrocketing electric bills. Instead, she's still paying several hundred dollars a month, and she says she ended up with a loan she never agreed to. "My best friend has such a program where she didn't pay a cent and she doesn't have a loan for her panels, and her electric bill is about $20," Wynter said. Wynter and her friend live in the same city and use the same technology, but ended up with completely different outcomes. The difference is who they hired. New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP) Commissioner Samuel Levine said he thinks solar panels can be a great deal, but he advises consumers to slow down. - . Advice for going solar "There are a lot of ways these companies try to trick consumers. Often, for example, they'll quote the monthly cost instead of the full cost," Levine said. The city's consumer watchdog says know the full price of the system: - Be skeptical of free solar claims - Understand what tax credits you actually qualify for: federal, state and city - Shop financing on your own - Don't assume the company's lender is your cheapest option "Often, what's involved there is the solar contractors getting kickbacks," Levine said. If you sign and something feels off, New York law gives you three business days to cancel with no penalty. "If you feel you might have been scammed or you're just not sure, exercise that right," Levine said. Send that cancellation notice in writing. Certified mail is the safest option. A few more tips: make sure your installer is licensed with the DCWP; get at least three written quotes and read every page, and if someone shows up at your door with an iPad and says the offer expires soon, it's not a deadline, it's a warning.

articles.title: Baby among 3 dead in holiday horror as Easter egg hunt turns deadly
articles.author: FOX News
articles.article_text: --- title: Baby among 3 dead in holiday horror as Easter egg hunt turns deadly author: FOX News url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/baby-among-3-dead-holiday-horror-easter-egg-hunt-turns-deadly hostname: fox5ny.com description: Three people were killed, including a 10-month-old girl, after high winds toppled a tree in Germany during an Easter egg hunt on Sunday morning, according to authorities. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-06 tags: ['World,News'] --- Three people were killed, including a 10-month-old girl, after high winds toppled a tree in Germany during an Easter egg hunt on Sunday morning, according to authorities. Around 50 people from a nearby residential facility for new mothers, pregnant women and children were attending the egg hunt in a wooded area near the town of Satrupholm at about 11 a.m. when a 100-foot tree fell on top of them, police said in a statement. Four people became pinned under the tree, police said. First responders arrived at the scene and first began treating a 21-year-old woman and a 16-year-old girl, but both died at the scene. The woman's 10-month-old daughter also later died at the hospital. An 18-year-old woman sustained serious injuries and was rushed to the hospital in a helicopter. The residential facility is part of the state-funded child welfare system, supporting pregnant women and new mothers who need help, according to its website. FILE - A chocolate Easter egg lies under a fallen tree in a wooded area south-east of Flensburg. Three people died in an accident on Easter Sunday, including a mother and her baby. (Photo by Daniel Reinhardt/picture alliance via Getty Images) Grief counselors were sent to the scene after the fatal incident on Sunday. Pictures from the scene showed several Easter eggs scattered on the ground as two of the victims were seen covered in white sheets. The German weather service had put the area under a high winds warning. Officials from the Schleswig-Holstein region, where the facility is located, said they were "deeply shaken" by the Easter tragedy. "Our thoughts are with the family members of the dead, with the injured, and with everyone who had to experience this terrible occurrence," regional Governor Daniel Günther, Interior Minister Magdalena Finke, and Youth and Families Minister Aminata Touré said in a joint statement to the dpa news agency. The Associated Press contributed to this report.

articles.title: Video shows vandal smash windshields of 19 vehicles in Linden, N.J., but the vandalism didn't stop there
articles.author: Allen Devlin
articles.article_text: --- title: Video shows vandal smash windshields of 19 vehicles in Linden, N.J., but the vandalism didn't stop there author: Allen Devlin url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/union-county-new-jersey-windshields-smashed-linden-cranford/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Police in New Jersey are searching for a vandal who threw bricks through the windows of vehicles parked on streets in several Union County towns. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['New Jersey, Vandalism'] --- Police in New Jersey are searching for a vandal who threw bricks through the windows of vehicles parked on streets in at least two Union County towns. Why the suspect did it remains a mystery. Windshield after windshield smashed in Linden Surveillance video from a Linden apartment complex shows a person in the early hours of Monday morning drive up, get out of his vehicle and throw bricks through the windshields of parked cars, chucking them aimlessly from multiple launch points. In all, 19 cars were struck, totaling thousands of dollars in damage, police said. Dozens of people were left to deal with considerable destruction. "I've seen a lot of crazy stuff. This is something that has never happened before," resident Dan Sawrcki said. He was referring to shattered windshield after shattered windshield after shattered windshield. "Fella drove in, threw bricks through windows," Sawrcki said. Judy Sinfleui said her mom's car was one of the many attacked, adding the money to fix it will have to come from out of pocket. "She's just in shock, just cause someone felt like throwing bricks," Sinfleui said. "I don't really understand what the motivation is for this" The vandalism spree didn't stop in Linden. Police said it happened in other towns throughout Union County, including in Cranford, where seemingly the same person threw rocks that damaged parked cars on the street, police said. Neighbors were left counting how many were hit. "It doesn't make me feel great," resident Scott Godfrey said. "It wasn't just us. It was everybody," resident Maryann Matyas said. Police are now working to track down the suspect from the videos. Residents, meanwhile, were left sweeping up shattered glass. "I don't really understand what the motivation is for this. Why would you smash a window and then drive on? That doesn't make any sense," Godfrey said.

articles.title: Country star Ray Stevens sustains broken neck after fall in Nashville
articles.author: Jessica Schladebeck
articles.article_text: --- title: Country star Ray Stevens sustains broken neck after fall in Nashville author: Jessica Schladebeck url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/07/country-star-ray-stevens-sustains-broken-neck-after-fall-in-nashville/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Country music star Ray Stevens is recovering at his home in Nashville after he suffered a fall and broke his neck over the weekend, according to his reports. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['SNYDE'] --- Country music star Ray Stevens is recovering at his home in Nashville after he suffered a fall and broke his neck over the weekend, according to reports. In a press release obtained by TMZ, Stevens’ team confirmed that the 87-year-old Grammy winner took a major tumble on Sunday and that he was “briefly hospitalized in the Nashville area.” He has since been advised to wear a neck brace for the next four weeks or so. “Despite the injury, he remains fully mobile and in good spirits as he continues his recovery,” his team said in a statement Tuesday. Details regarding the circumstances of Stevens’ fall were unclear. The incident comes just days before the slated release of his forthcoming album, Favorites Old & New. It will still be available to fans on Friday, as scheduled, his team told People. The 13-track collection “features a curated mix of beloved standards alongside new selections from a range of talented songwriters,” according to the release. “I had a lot of fun creating this album, ‘Favorites Old & New,'” Stevens said. “It really does contain a few of my favorite old songs as well as favorite new ones penned by some talented writers. I just hope Ray Stevens fans enjoy it as much as Ray Stevens!” A comedic country music singer and songwriter, Stevens shot to the fame in the 1970s and 80s, penning songs over the course of his career with superstars like Dolly Parton and Elvis Presley. In 2025, he suffered a mild heart attack. He was hospitalized after experiencing chest pain, and he underwent a minimally invasive heart catheterization procedure at the time. After a follow-up procedure — during which doctors placed a pair of stents in his heart — and some rehab, Stevens wrapped his recovery at home. Some of his hits include “The Shriners Convention,” “The Streak” and the 1970 Grammy-winning bop, “Everything Is Beautiful.”

articles.title: Expert warns "AI is being weaponized" as spam calls get more deceptive during tax season
articles.author: Tim McNicholas; Walter Smith Randolph
articles.article_text: --- title: Expert warns "AI is being weaponized" as spam calls get more deceptive during tax season author: Tim McNicholas; Walter Smith Randolph url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/ai-spam-calls-tax-season/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: The technology behind spam calls is getting more sophisticated, an expert with the TrueCaller app says. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-03-19 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Artificial Intelligence'] --- If you're getting barraged with spam calls during tax season, you're not alone. It's one of the busiest times for these schemes as people prey on stressed out taxpayers. But if doing your taxes correctly isn't stressful enough this year, experts say is making spam calls trickier to track.They can "synthesize an American accent" Clayton LiaBraaten with the spam call blocking app TrueCaller says the technology is getting more sophisticated. "AI is being weaponized against the consumer. So now I can have better scripts, I can , even if I'm calling from Eastern Europe or East Asia. So it's a very busy time of year for us to try to protect consumers and our users," LiaBraaten said.In 2023, Suffolk County Police told CBS News New York Investigates that an alleged scammer used AI to mimic a real girl's voice into paying thousands of dollars.The technology has only improved since then, as people get spam calls about everything from their taxes to social security benefits. "Scams really can effect anyone. We just noticed in the data that the elder population was losing the most amount of money. In some instances, they were losing their entire life savings," said Rebecca Rose, with the Social Security Administration Office of the Inspector General. "We fight AI with AI" Apps such as Robokiller, Hiya and TrueCaller, work to identify spam callers and then block them or warn users. "We fight AI with AI. We're detecting the synthesized voices, we're detecting the scams, we're detecting the high-volume calls and making sure that our users are protected," LiaBraaten said. Federal government agencies, especially the IRS, tend to communicate via the mail and will rarely, if ever, call you. If you ever have doubt about whether you're actually speaking with a government agency, you can always look up the agency's phone number and reach out directly.

articles.title: Colorado QB Dominiq Ponder was 2x over legal limit before fatal crash: report
articles.author: Brian Niemietz
articles.article_text: --- title: Colorado QB Dominiq Ponder was 2x over legal limit before fatal crash: report author: Brian Niemietz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/07/colorado-quarteback-dominiq-ponder-death-drunk-driving-coroner-report/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Colorado quarterback Dominiq Ponder’s autopsy report shows the 23-year-old athlete’s blood alcohol level was twice the legal limit prior to fatal crash, a report claims. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['National News'] tags: ['Colorado quarterback, Dominiq Ponder, accident, crash, drunk driving, 23, football'] --- University of Colorado quarterback Dominiq Ponder drove with twice the legal limit of alcohol in his system prior to dying in a rollover crash last month. An autopsy report shows the 23-year-old athlete’s blood-alcohol content was 0.167 g/100 mL, according to USA Today. He died around 3 a.m. on March 1 after reportedly losing control of his 2023 Tesla in Boulder, Co. Authorities said Ponder’s car crashed through a guardrail and hit an electrical pole. It reportedly flipped over at least once. A coroner’s report called Ponder a “restrained driver involved in a single vehicle collision at a high rate of speed.” Multiple blunt force injuries were his cause of death. Ponder planned to begin spring practice the following day. He was to wear the number 7 for the first time having previously sported the number 22 on his jersey. His family launched the Dominiq Ponder 722 Foundation to honor the college junior. That initiative aims to “promote responsible decision-making” as well as supporting children’s hospitals and families facing medical issues. Ponder’s mom, Catrina Hughes, told USA Today her son’s death should remind young people to think before they act. “If anything good can come from this loss, it’s the conversations it can start about responsible decision-making, supporting young adults, and making good choices even in ordinary moments,” she said. The Colorado Buffaloes honored Ponder for “his life, his spirit, and the light he brought to Boulder” in a statement following the Florida native’s short life. Colorado’s outspoken coach, former NFL star Deion Sanders, told reporters his team will honor Ponder by wearing a patch on their uniforms during the upcoming football season.

articles.title: NASA's next Artemis missions being planned with help of N.Y. professor: "I was over the moon!"
articles.author: Jennifer McLogan
articles.article_text: --- title: NASA's next Artemis missions being planned with help of N.Y. professor: "I was over the moon!" author: Jennifer McLogan url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/artemis-nasa-future-moon-missions-timothy-glotch/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Stony Brook University professor Timothy Glotch is one of 10 scientists selected to shape research plans for NASA's next Artemis missions. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-03 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Suffolk County, NASA, Featured Stories & Web Exclusives'] --- Shortly after the on its trip around the moon, a college professor in New York learned he was picked by NASA to help with future missions.Stony Brook University professor Timothy Glotch is one of just 10 scientists from across the country selected to shape research plans for the next .The call of a lifetime Glotch got the call of his life , when NASA exploration leaders told the Long Island planetary geologist that he and nine others would be bringing their wealth of experience to the ."I was over the moon!" he said. "My heart was racing, I couldn't wipe the smile off my face for the entire day." Glotch will help astronauts on the 2028 Artemis mission locate minerals and water ice near the moon's south pole, and start to build a base on the moon in the coming years. "Artemis is being built from the ground up to be sustainable," he said. "I'm excited to bring Artemis to Stony Brook and teach classes." Planning next moon launch Glotch will be front and center for the 2028 launch to the moon from either Cape Canaveral, Florida, or the Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas. While discussing his elation, Glotch donned gloves - a strict protocol - and showed lunar dust experiments from rock samples collected on the surface of the 4.5 billion-year-old moon by Apollo astronauts more than 50 years ago. "We can take this tiny little amount of sample and use it to inform our global perspective," he said. "By studying the moon, we learn more about the Earth." All of his research is being done on Earth, but would Glotch ever want to take a rocket on a trip to the moon? "I don't even like tall roller coasters," he said. "I'm happy to do my science here on Earth."

articles.title: Mamdani administration releases NYC's 1st Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living reports
articles.author: Jesse Zanger
articles.article_text: --- title: Mamdani administration releases NYC's 1st Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living reports author: Jesse Zanger url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/nyc-preliminary-citywide-racial-equity-plan-true-cost-of-living-reports/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released New York City's first Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living Measure. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Zohran Mamdani'] --- Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released New York City's first Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living Measure. The reports were mandated by .The Mamdani administration says the two reports "make clear that New York City's affordability crisis is deeply tied to its history of racial inequity," and cited "patterns of disinvestment, exclusion from homeownership, unequal access to health care" and more as factors. "This Preliminary Racial Equity Plan is the first step in developing a whole-of-government approach to tackling that reality. It is a plan that lays out these first steps to solve decades of neglect and discrimination, and it places the work of 45 city agencies within a singular framework," Mamdani said. "Inequity has been embedded in the foundation of our city and nation since their inception; dismantling it requires a collective effort," said NYC Chief Equity Officer and NYC Mayor's Office of Equity & Racial Justice Commissioner Afua Atta-Mensah. Goals of the the Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan include advancing pay equity, anti-racism training for city government staff and improving data collection across various demographics. The TCOL report found 62% of New Yorkers don't make enough to cover their basic living expenses. The report found the median annual costs for a family with children are $159,197, but median resources are $124,007, meaning there's a greater than $35,000 gap. The reports come at a time when the in federal government.U.S. Assistant Attorney General for Civil Rights Harmeet Dhillon responded to the reports on social media. "Sounds fishy/illegal. Will review!" she wrote.

articles.title: Suffolk County police rescue teen stuck in mud at Wertheim National Wildlife Refuge
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Suffolk County police rescue teen stuck in mud at Wertheim National Wildlife Refuge author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/bodycam-video-shows-ny-teen-rescued-first-responders-getting-stuck-mud-suffolk-county-wildlife-refuge/18849393/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Police said 15-year-old Benjamin Moore inadvertently stepped into an offshoot of the Carmans River, where he sank into the mud and became stuck. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['disasters-accidents'] tags: ['18849393', 'rescue,suffolk-county-news,Shirley,Suffolk-County'] --- SHIRLEY, New York (WABC) -- A 15-year-old boy was rescued after becoming stuck in mud while lost in a wooded area of Suffolk County Sunday night, according to police. Officers responded to a 911 call at around 7:27 p.m. from Benjamin Moore, who said he had gone for a walk inside Wertheim National Wildlife Refuge in Shirley and could not find his way back after dark. Police said Moore accidentally stepped into an offshoot of the Carmans River, where he sank into the mud and became stuck. Body camera video shows officers working to locate the teen as he remained on the phone with a 911 dispatcher. He was instructed to use the flashlight on his cellphone to signal for help. Suffolk County Police Aviation Section officers searched from above while Moore waved his phone's light to guide them. Once he was spotted, aviation crews directed officers on the ground to his location. Patrol officers and Emergency Service Section members were able to reach Moore and pull him out of the muddy water at around 8:25 p.m., about an hour after he called. Additional support from the Canine Section, Marine Bureau, and local fire departments assisted in the response. Moore was escorted out of the woods and transported by ambulance to Stony Brook University Hospital for evaluation. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Fireworks may have caused portable toilet explosion in Yonkers park: police
articles.author: Marcus Solis
articles.article_text: --- title: Fireworks may have caused portable toilet explosion in Yonkers park: police author: Marcus Solis url: https://abc7ny.com/post/portable-toilet-explodes-yonkers-bregano-park-police-believe-fireworks-may-have-caused-blast/18853209/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The destruction, which occurred around 8:20 p.m. in Bregano Park, prompted a significant response, complete with agents in protective suits collecting evidence. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18853209', 'explosion,toilet,police,FBI,Westchester-County,Yonkers'] --- YONKERS, New York (WABC) -- Police are investigating after a portable toilet exploded in a park in Yonkers Monday night. The destruction, which occurred around 8:20 p.m. in Bregano Park, prompted a significant response, complete with agents in protective suits collecting evidence. The entire perimeter of the park has been cordoned off. The focus of attention is a blue porta potty that has now been blasted to pieces. They believe whoever did it used fireworks. "Last night, it sounded like a bomb," said resident Maria Sigismondo. "I was sitting in the living room and I went, 'oh my god.' The whole floor shook. And then my neighbors, you know, and everybody were just talking about it. Residents in the area say there have been four or five similar explosions over the last few weeks, usually around the same time after nightfall. "At first, we thought it was like fireworks. Then it got louder and bigger, but last night was really big," said resident Kenny Reyes. "And now this." Yonkers Police contacted the county bomb squad which responded along with the FBI and ATF. "I am pretty concerned, especially when you see the FBI, but hopefully they get to the bottom of it. You know, you worry about it," Sigismondo said. "The world today is a little strange." ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

It will be another cold night with temperatures in the mid-30s. On Thursday, we'll get off to a cold start, but it will be a more of a pleasant afternoon despite still being a little bit below average. It will be nice on Friday, with temperatures in the mid-60s. We'll still be in the 60s on Saturday, but there is a cold front working through, so morning clouds and a spot shower give way to sunshine on Sunday with temperatures in the lower 60s. Wednesday Chilly despite sun. High 50. Thursday Cold start, warmer PM. High 54. Friday Warmer and nice. High 65. Saturday AM shower? Brightening. High 66. Sunday Sun and PM clouds. High 62. Monday Cloudier, but warmer. High 75. Tuesday Summery. High 80. Please fill out the form below to submit weather photos and videos. articles.title: Man, 49, repeatedly stabbed, pepper-sprayed in random Chinatown attack
articles.author: Nicholas Williams; Thomas Tracy
articles.article_text: --- title: Man, 49, repeatedly stabbed, pepper-sprayed in random Chinatown attack author: Nicholas Williams; Thomas Tracy url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/07/man-49-repeatedly-stabbed-pepper-sprayed-in-random-chinatown-attack/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: The victim was walking past the Wai Wah Market on Catherine St. near Broadway around 9:30 a.m. when a stranger dressed in a blue tracksuit stormed up to him and started arguing with him, police and witnesses said. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['Crime and Public Safety'] --- A 49-year-old man was repeatedly stabbed and doused with pepper spray by a stranger in an unprovoked attack on a Chinatown street on Tuesday, police said. The victim was walking past the Wai Wah Market on Catherine St. near East Broadway around 9:30 a.m. when the stranger, who was dressed in a blue tracksuit, stormed up and started arguing with him, police and witnesses said. “They were walking the same direction and one guy sounded like they said, ‘Why you looking at me?’ or something, and then it just started,” Joanne Hung, a cashier at the Wai Wah Market, told the Daily News. “I heard the noise (from the argument).” As the confrontation continued, the suspect pulled a knife and a can of pepper spray, witnesses said. He repeatedly stabbed his victim in the arm and back before spraying the man in the eyes, Hung said. “The other guy who cut him had pepper spray,” she said. “The pepper spray was really strong.” After the suspect ran off, the victim went into the market to ask for water to clean out his eyes, Hung said. EMS took him to Bellevue Hospital, where he was expected to survive. His attacker ran off and was still being sought by authorities Tuesday. “The victim, he was conscious, he was talking. He said he doesn’t know that guy,” Hung said. “This is the first time something like this has happened. It was early morning, so it wasn’t a lot of people outside.”

articles.title: Olympian Alyssa Liu wants to become a professional piercer
articles.author: Brian Niemietz
articles.article_text: --- title: Olympian Alyssa Liu wants to become a professional piercer author: Brian Niemietz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/03/17/olympian-alyssa-liu-wants-to-become-a-professional-piercer/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Gold medal winning figure skater Alyssa Liu hopes to get a license in piercing. The 20-year-old Olympian has already pierced her own ear and mouth. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-03-17 categories: ['National News'] tags: ['Alyssa Liu, piercing, Olympics, figure skater, gold medal, Vanity Fair'] --- Olympic gold medalist Alyssa Liu plans to get licensed to pierce people. The 20-year-old figure skater confessed she’s already pierced herself multiple times and at least one other person as well. “I did my own helix and my third lobes too,” Liu said in a video shot while she prepared to attend Sunday’s Vanity Fair Oscar party. “I actually want to get licensed.” Liu proudly showed holes she put at the top and bottom of her ear lobes as well as other ear piercings. Asked if she’s pierced anyone else, she coyly held her index finger over her lips, nodded and grinned. Liu told Vanity Fair that her unique style is inspired by her siblings, most notably her brother Justin, with whom she shares clothing. Three of those siblings are triplets, according to People. She showed off the earrings she planned to wear the party, then addressed her “smiley” — a dental piercing she previously discussed while participating in last month’s Milano Cortina Olympic games. “I had my sister hold-up my lip and I was, like, looking in the mirror and I had my piercing needle… and yeah, I just put it through,” Liu told NBC affiliate TMJ4 . She said that particular piercing, which everyone saw when Liu smiled during the Olympic games, is about 2 years old. Liu became one of the brightest stars of that competition by winning gold in a personal and team event just four years after announcing her retirement from figure skating.

articles.title: What day will it feel most like spring in the NYC area?
articles.author: Amanda Hurley
articles.article_text: --- title: What day will it feel most like spring in the NYC area? author: Amanda Hurley url: https://www.fox5ny.com/weather/what-day-will-feel-most-like-spring-nyc-area hostname: fox5ny.com description: Spring may have begun last month, but winter weather continues to pop up in forecasts across the country. So, when does it truly arrive in the NYC area? sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-06 tags: ['Us,Ny,Nyc,Weather'] --- NEW YORK CITY - Spring may have begun last month, but winter weather continues to pop up in forecasts across the country. So, when does spring truly arrive? It depends on where you live! What we know: A recent study identified the "springiest" day of the year for each state based on the last spring freeze for each region, along with when greenery starts to peak. The New York City area won't get that spring feeling until May! In New Jersey, it will feel the most like spring on May 5, with New York just days behind on May 7. However, spring will seem far away for both states this week as temperatures dip into the 40s and 50s during the day, and 30s at night! Big picture view: The "springiest" day ranges from as early as February 8 in southern Texas, to as late as May 31 in Wyoming. In other northeastern states, Virginia has already reached its spring peak on April 5, while Pennsylvania will feel the most like spring on April 19, followed by Maryland on April 20 and Delaware on April 21. What you can do: Knowing when spring truly arrives across the country can help you plan when and where to travel throughout the season. According to the study, "early spring trips in March tend to favor the South and Gulf states. By April, comfortable conditions shift toward the Midwest and East Coast. And by late April or early May, some of the most pleasant spring weather occurs in the northern states and those at higher elevations." The Source: Information from this article was sourced from Exoticaa.

articles.title: Thieves target luxury cars in Hell's Kitchen garage, 1 crashes nearby
articles.author: Isabel Soisson
articles.article_text: --- title: Thieves target luxury cars in Hell's Kitchen garage, 1 crashes nearby author: Isabel Soisson url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/thieves-target-luxury-cars-hells-kitchen-garage-1-crashes-nearby hostname: fox5ny.com description: Police are searching for suspects after a morning heist at a Hell's Kitchen parking garage targeted multiple high-end vehicles. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-05 tags: ['Crime Publicsafety,News,Us,Ny,Us,Ny,Nyc,Manhattan'] --- NEW YORK - Police are searching for suspects after a morning heist at a Hell's Kitchen parking garage targeted multiple high-end vehicles. McLaren crashes, Range Rover stuck as suspects flee What we know: According to police, the incident occurred just before 6 a.m. at a garage on West 43rd Street near the West Side Highway. Four men entered the garage around 5:45 a.m. and attempted to steal several luxury cars, including a blue McLaren sports car and a black Mercedes‑Benz G‑Wagon. Police say one suspect drove the McLaren out of the garage but crashed it into a nearby pole shortly afterward. The vehicle has since been recovered. Inside the garage, a parking attendant lowered the metal security gate to block the thieves, striking a white Range Rover being driven by one of the suspects. Police report the SUV became stuck and damaged under the barrier. A light-colored Volvo in the garage also sustained significant damage, according to authorities. Police say the suspects ultimately fled the area in a gray BMW that was not stolen from the garage. What we don't know: No arrests have been made, and suspect descriptions have not been released. No injuries were reported. Police continue to review surveillance footage and collect evidence, and ask anyone with information to contact the New York Police Department. The Source: Information from the NYPD.

articles.title: Vigil held Saturday for 7-month-old Kaori Patterson-Moore killed in Brooklyn shooting
articles.author: Tyler Thrasher
articles.article_text: --- title: Vigil held Saturday for 7-month-old Kaori Patterson-Moore killed in Brooklyn shooting author: Tyler Thrasher url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/vigil-held-saturday-7-month-old-kaori-patterson-moore-killed-brooklyn-shooting hostname: fox5ny.com description: A Brooklyn community gathered Saturday to honor 7-month-old Kaori Patterson-Moore after her death in a shooting. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-04 tags: ['Crime Publicsafety,News,Us,Ny'] --- NEW YORK - A grieving Brooklyn community came together to honor a baby whose life was cut tragically short on Saturday. The vigil was organized by NYC clergy, the Brooklyn Collective Clergy Council and the United Clergy Council. The gathering followed the death of 7-month-old Kaori Patterson-Moore, who was fatally shot earlier this week in Brooklyn. Organizers say the vigil was intended as a time for prayer, reflection and community support. File Photo. The backstory: Kaori Patterson-Moore was shot while sitting in a stroller during a daytime shooting in Williamsburg, according to police. Authorities say two people on a moped opened fire on a group, striking the infant, who later died from her injuries. Police believe the child was not the intended target. A suspect, Amuri Greene, has been charged with murder, and another suspect has also been taken into custody, according to authorities. What they're saying: "This tragedy has deeply shaken our community, and our hearts are heavy with grief," organizers said. "This should never be happening, and it is incredibly sad. Now more than ever, we must come together as a community united in prayer, worship and compassion," they added. The Source: This article was written using information from previous FOX 5 NY reporting and a community press release from local clergy organizations.

articles.title: The Big Deal
articles.article_text: --- title: The Big Deal url: https://spectrumlocalnews.com/section/the-big-deal hostname: spectrumlocalnews.com description: In Spectrum News' new weekly show "The Big Deal," Errol Louis takes a look at the most important developments from the campaign trail, and dives into real-world issues motivating and impacting voters. sitename: spectrumlocalnews.com date: 2024-02-29 tags: ['Politics, APP National Politics, Nationwide'] --- To make informed choices, voters need to hear about the issues from a trusted source. That’s where “The Big Deal with Errol Louis” comes in. Every week, watch in-depth analysis and informed conversation from Errol and from the best of Spectrum News' political journalists across the country as they break down the most important issues shaping our nation and the politics involved. Errol Louis has hosted "Inside City Hall" on Spectrum News NY1 for more than a decade. The nightly prime-time show about New York City politics features interviews with top political and cultural leaders. Louis is also a longtime CNN contributor, providing on-air commentary on key events, including presidential primaries and election night. He writes regularly for CNN.com, as well as a weekly column for New York Magazine on a range of political and social affairs. He was recently ranked No. 36 on the list of the 100 most powerful people in New York City politics. Louis is an adjunct professor of Urban Reporting at the Craig Newmark Graduate School of Journalism at CUNY, where he has taught political and investigative reporting to more than 100 graduate students. He is co-editor of Deadline Artists, a two-volume anthology of America’s greatest newspaper columns published in October 2011.

articles.title: The Republican trying to fill Sherrill's vacant Congress seat: Meet Joe Hathaway
articles.author: Briana Scalia
articles.article_text: --- title: The Republican trying to fill Sherrill's vacant Congress seat: Meet Joe Hathaway author: Briana Scalia url: https://www.fox5ny.com/election/joe-hathaway-nj-special-election-mikie-sherrill-congress hostname: fox5ny.com description: The special election to fill the seat in Congress that Gov. Mikie Sherrill left vacant is swiftly approaching. Here's the Republican candidate hoping to supersede her. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-06 tags: ['Politics,Election,Us,Nj'] --- NEW JERSEY - The special election to fill the seat in Congress that Gov. Mikie Sherrill left vacant is swiftly approaching. Here's the Republican candidate hoping to supersede her. Meet Joe Hathaway What we know: Former Mayor of Randolph Joe Hathaway is the Republican nominee running for New Jersey's 11th congressional district. Hathaway is a self-described "lifelong New Jersey resident" who served on Randolph's Township Council before becoming the mayor. In an interview with Fox News, Hathaway said that "the district is ready for a new generation of leadership." The New York Post published their endorsement of the former mayor last month. Issues, platform If elected to Congress, Hathaway's foremost promise on his campaign website is to eliminate taxes for first-time home buyers. He also mentions lowering healthcare costs by cutting out "unnecessary middlemen" and investing in transportation, specifically citing the Gateway Tunnel. Hathaway is also a proponent of supporting both law enforcement and enforcement at the country's borders. The Source: This article includes information from Joe Hathaway's campaign website.

The infant was fatally shot earlier this week while sitting in a stroller during a daytime shooting in Williamsburg. Police say she was not the intended target. Two suspects are now in custody, including one charged with murder. At the vigil, loved ones and community members gathered for prayer and reflection, with Kaori’s family making an emotional plea to end violence and protect children. Organizers say the gathering was meant to bring unity, healing and support as the community mourns the devastating loss.

articles.title: Letters to the Editor: 04.09.26
articles.author: Jackie Meyer
articles.article_text: --- title: Letters to the Editor: 04.09.26 author: Jackie Meyer url: https://www.ourtownny.com/voices/letters-to-the-editor-040926-AA5727882 hostname: ourtownny.com description: Thank you, Mr. Patel. âNeither snow, nor rain, nor heat, nor gloom of night stays these couriers from the swift completion of their appointed rounds.â ---Post O... sitename: www.ourtownny.com date: 2026-04-03 tags: ['jackie meyer', 'postal worker retirement'] --- Letters to the Editor: 04.09.26 Thank you Mr. Patel. A resident thanks a postman who faithfully delivered mail for nearly 20 years. Thank you, Mr. Patel. âNeither snow, nor rain, nor heat, nor gloom of night stays these couriers from the swift completion of their appointed rounds.â ---Post Office Motto This motto rings especially true in todaysâ world of climate extremes. For close to 20 years, my building has been lucky to be situated on the route of mailman, Mr. Patel. With a smile and encouraging word, between the hours of 2 to 3 p.m., we could always depend on his accurate delivery of our ever decreasing (yet anxiously awaited!) letters, catalogues, and bills. So very few things can be depended on these days, that it truly came as a blessing to know that one thing always could be.....Mr. Patel delivering the mail. We wish him all the best in his well deserved retirement! We will miss him. âGuess itâs just gonna have to be email. A grateful Asten House resident.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Pakistan asks Trump for deadline extension on Iran threat
articles.article_text: --- title: Iran live updates: Pakistan asks Trump for deadline extension on Iran threat url: https://abc7ny.com/live-updates/iran-war-strait-hormuz-ceasefire-trump-stock-market/18847792/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: President Donald Trump is threatening to destroy all of Iran's bridges and power plants in a four-hour blitz attack on Tuesday night if the countries don't agree to a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. sitename: ABC OTV Website date: 2026-04-07 tags: ['18847792'] --- Iranian-affiliated cyber actors are targeting U.S. critical infrastructure, according to a new warning from the federal government. Numerous agencies, including the FBI and NSA, "are urgently warning U.S. organizations of ongoing cyber exploitation of internet-connected operational technology (OT) devices, including Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley-manufactured programmable logic controllers (PLCs), across multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors," according to an alert published on Tuesday. Multiple critical infrastructure sectors experienced disruptions, and in some cases "this activity has resulted in operational disruption and financial loss," the alert said. The Iranian-backed group has targeted government services industry, water and wastewater systems and the energy sector, according to the agencies. Iranian-backed actors have carried out similar actions in the past, according to the government.

articles.title: Passenger gives birth midair on flight from Jamaica to New York City
articles.author: Amanda Hurley
articles.article_text: --- title: Passenger gives birth midair on flight from Jamaica to New York City author: Amanda Hurley url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/passenger-gives-birth-midair-flight-to-new-york-city hostname: fox5ny.com description: A pregnant woman boarded her flight to New York City this weekend as a party of one, but touched down on the tarmac as a family of two. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-07 tags: ['Us,Ny,Nyc,News,Lifestyle,Travel'] --- NEW YORK CITY - A pregnant woman boarded her flight to New York City this weekend as a party of one, but touched down on the tarmac as a family of two. What we know: A passenger gave birth midair during a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston, Jamaica to New York City on Saturday. The nearly 4-hour flight landed at JFK airport just before noon as pilots alerted air traffic controllers that they had welcomed a baby onboard! The airline says no emergency was declared during the flight, and the mother and newborn received medical care upon arrival. What we don't know: The identities of the mother and baby have not been released. What they're saying: However, air traffic controllers had their own idea for a possible baby name with a nod to JFK airport. "Tell her she’s got to name it Kennedy," the controller said in audio obtained by CBS News. The Source: Information from this article was sourced from Caribbean Airlines and CBS News.

articles.title: Submit a tip to the CBS New York Investigative Team
articles.author: Walter Smith Randolph
articles.article_text: --- title: Submit a tip to the CBS New York Investigative Team author: Walter Smith Randolph url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/submit-a-tip-to-the-cbs-new-york-investigative-team/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: A great investigative news tip should explain the situation in detail, be newsworthy, and have real-world consequences. sitename: CBS New York date: 2023-12-26 categories: ['Local News'] --- Do you have a story that needs investigating? If so, fill out the form below, or leave us a message at 646-939-6095 or email cbs2investigates@cbs.com. We can also be reached using Telegram or WhatsApp--just search the number above. Please be sure to leave contact information in case we have follow-up questions. Your contact information and identity will not be shared beyond our team or used in our reporting. A great investigative news tip should explain the situation in detail, be newsworthy, and have real-world consequences. Please be very specific about the problem and include any documents or evidence you might have. While we understand you may want to be anonymous, we still may need to speak with someone to get more information and help point us in the right direction. We appreciate you sharing your investigative story idea. Due to the high volume of tips, we only respond to tips we are interested in pursuing.

articles.title: Tiny bear cub rescued by troopers along NJ interstate
articles.author: Amanda Hurley
articles.article_text: --- title: Tiny bear cub rescued by troopers along NJ interstate author: Amanda Hurley url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/tiny-bear-cub-rescued-troopers-along-nj-interstate hostname: fox5ny.com description: If being too adorable was a crime, this latest police rescue would be a No. 1 suspect! sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-06 tags: ['Us,Nj,News,Heartwarming'] --- UNION TOWNSHIP. N.J. - If being too adorable was a crime, this latest police rescue would be a No. 1 suspect! What we know: An abandoned bear cub was spotted in a roadside ditch along I-78 in Union Township last week. NJ State Police troopers said the lone bear needed help, and brought it back to the barracks before releasing it to the Department of Environmental Protection. Pictures posted by the department show a smiling trooper holding the adorable cub, along with another picture of the bear's police ridealong. What's next: Police say the bear is now safe and receiving the care he needs. What they're saying: The baby bear immediately captured hearts on social media, with many commenters saying the department should adopt the cub. "I mean, I'm thinking the latest recruit of the k9 unit," one commenter said. While another said to "put him through the academy." The Source: Information from this article was provided by NJ State Police.

articles.title: Man struck and killed at bus stop near Staten Island mall after driver loses control
articles.author: Colin Mixson
articles.article_text: --- title: Man struck and killed at bus stop near Staten Island mall after driver loses control author: Colin Mixson url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/06/man-struck-and-killed-at-bus-stop-near-staten-island-mall-after-driver-loses-control/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: The 51-year-old driver was heading south on Ring Rd. when he lost control of his 2015 Nissan Pathfinder and struck the victim at the bus stop just north of Platinum Ave. in New Springfield around midnight, according to law enforcement… sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Crime and Public Safety'] --- A 40-year-old man was killed after a driver lost control and plowed into a bus stop near the Staten Island mall early Monday. The 51-year-old driver was heading south on Ring Rd. around midnight when he lost control of his 2015 Nissan Pathfinder and struck the victim at the bus stop just north of Platinum Ave. in New Springfield, according to law enforcement officials. Medics rushed the victim to Staten Island University Hospital, where he died. The driver remained at the scene. There were no arrests. The bus shelter serving S44, S59 and S79 buses was heavily damaged in the crash, according to the Staten Island Advance.

articles.title: Duck Donuts closes in Selden
articles.author: Erica Marcus
articles.article_text: --- title: Duck Donuts closes in Selden author: Erica Marcus url: https://www.newsday.com/lifestyle/restaurants/duck-donuts-selden-l043y339 hostname: newsday.com description: The national chain still has four franchise locations on Long Island sitename: Newsday date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Things to Do'] tags: ['Restaurants, FeedMe, FMClose'] --- There are more than 100 Duck Donuts in 25 states and five countries but, now, one less on Long Island. The Selden shop, which opened in 2020 in the College Plaza shopping center, has closed. Callers were met with a voice mail message on Monday notifying shoppers of the closure. Owner Veronica Bencivenga could not immediately be reached for comment. In 2018, Veronica and her husband, Anthony Bencivenga, opened New York State’s first franchise of the North Carolina-based chain. That store, in Hauppauge, remains open. It's Omar Athar who has the franchise rights for Nassau County and operates Duck Donuts in East Meadow, Garden City and Plainview. Duck Donuts, famous for its endlessly customizable doughnuts, is named for Duck, North Carolina. It was in this beach destination on the state’s Outer Banks where the chain was founded by Russ DiGilio got its start in 2007.

articles.title: Civil lawsuit filed against Gilgo Beach suspect Rex Heuermann, 2 relatives
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Civil lawsuit filed against Gilgo Beach suspect Rex Heuermann, 2 relatives author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/gilgo-beach-murders-son-victim-valerie-mack-files-lawsuit-relatives-rex-heuermann/18850100/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Benjamin Torres, the grown son of murder victim Valerie Mack, is seeking money from Heuermann, his ex-wife and his daughter. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18850100', 'lawsuit,gilgo-beach,murder,Suffolk-County'] --- NEW YORK (WABC) -- A civil lawsuit has been filed against Gilgo Beach murder suspect Rex Heuermann, and two of his relatives. Benjamin Torres, the grown son of murder victim Valerie Mack, is seeking money from Heuermann, his ex-wife and his daughter. He cites the murder itself, saying Mack endured terror, restraint, pain and mutilation. Torres also wants money the Heuermanns could make from selling their story. He named Heuermann's daughter Victoria for dismissing the murders as "kink" behavior. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover?
